posts.text:

posts.summary: Iran exported 40 million barrels of crude oil between June 15-26, 2026, with half of that volume shipped on a single day (June 19), indicating a significant spike in export activity. The concentrated export surge could reflect efforts to move oil quickly, possibly due to market or geopolitical pressures.

posts.text:
Oil Tanker Booked in Gulf at 897% of Benchmark Freight Rate

 A supertanker has been provisionally booked to transport oil from the Persian Gulf to India at a rate equivalent to almost nine times benchmark freight costs, an eye-watering price that reflects the shortage of
posts.summary: A supertanker has been booked to transport oil from the Persian Gulf to India at nearly nine times the benchmark freight rate, indicating an acute shortage of available shipping capacity. The extraordinarily high rate reflects significant supply-demand imbalances in the tanker market that could increase oil transportation costs and potentially affect global crude prices.

posts.summary: Exxon is set to halt its largest European refinery due to a strike, which could reduce refined product supply in European markets and potentially tighten global oil product balances. This disruption may create upward pressure on fuel prices in the region depending on the duration and scope of the shutdown.

posts.text:
Hormuz traffic cautiously improves

Confirmed Strait of Hormuz traffic stayed active on 23 June, with 31 verified crossings across commercial and energy-linked vessels. West-to-east movements dominated, while Iranian, Omani and IMO routes all remained in use. The Strait appears https://t.co/TOe354A89q
posts.summary: Strait of Hormuz traffic remained active on June 23 with 31 verified vessel crossings, suggesting continued oil and energy flows through one of the world's most critical maritime chokepoints. The sustained traffic indicates maintained supply routes despite regional tensions.

posts.text:
EIA inventory report:

U.S. commercial crude inventories fell by 6 million barrels, while the Strategic Petroleum Reserve declined by a further 9 million barrels to a fresh multi-decade low. Combined, total U.S. crude stockpiles have fallen by around 100 million barrels since https://t.co/YYb4R7ie68
posts.summary: U.S. crude inventories have declined significantly, with commercial stockpiles down 6 million barrels and the Strategic Petroleum Reserve dropping to multi-decade lows, reflecting tightening supply conditions that could support oil prices. The combined 100 million barrel reduction in total U.S. crude stockpiles suggests sustained demand or production constraints pressuring available supplies.

posts.text: Trump is now turning his attention to refiners and fuel producers, criticizing the slow pace of declines in retail gasoline prices following the sharp drop in crude oil. However, the refining sector's response remains constrained by historically low inventories of refined https://t.co/qPgOM4srBS https://t.co/lyha5bPb9A
posts.summary: Trump is criticizing fuel refiners for not reducing retail gasoline prices quickly enough despite sharp declines in crude oil costs. The refining sector's ability to respond is limited by historically low inventories of refined products.

posts.text: WTI crude oil $69.96/bbl... if this was fall/autumn we'd eventually see the national average $3.25/gal- but RFG/summer gasoline specs and strong crack spreads will mean for now the national average might eventually drop into the mid-$3s. a sub-$3 run is possible around November.
posts.summary: WTI crude oil at $69.96/barrel could push the national gas average into the mid-$3s currently due to summer gasoline specifications and strong crack spreads, with potential for sub-$3 prices by November. The seasonal shift from summer to winter fuel blends is expected to provide additional downward pressure on gas prices.

posts.text: For just the third time ever, PADD 2 saw weekly back-to-back refinery utilization rates over 100%
posts.summary: PADD 2 (a U.S. refining region) recently achieved weekly refinery utilization rates exceeding 100% for only the third time on record, indicating exceptionally tight refining capacity and potentially constrained gasoline and diesel supply in that region.

posts.text:
Hormuz traffic cautiously improves

The Strait of Hormuz continued to show signs of operational recovery on 23 June, with 31 verified crossings recorded across a broad mix of vessel types, including #crude, chemicals, container, bulk and general cargo traffic. West-to-East https://t.co/ZIsVhezO9j
posts.summary: The Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil supplies, showed signs of operational recovery on June 23 with 31 verified vessel crossings including crude oil traffic. The improvement in transit activity suggests reduced disruptions to oil flows through one of the world's most important energy shipping routes.

posts.text:
Oil investment of $17.7 trillion required to meet demand in the long term.

Total cumulative oil investments to meet demand requirements add up to $17.7 trillion
(US$2026) for the period 2026–2050. For the upstream, investment requirements are assessed at $14.5 trillion. https://t.co/lvRY7jPW3r
posts.summary: OPEC estimates that $17.7 trillion in cumulative oil investments will be needed between 2026 and 2050 to meet long-term global demand, with upstream development requiring $14.5 trillion of that total. This signals OPEC's view that substantial capital deployment will be essential to sustain oil supply over the coming decades.

posts.text:
Global energy spending is on track to reach $3.4 trillion in 2026

Around $2.2 trillion of this is set to go to grids, storage, low-emissions fuels, nuclear, renewables, efficiency & electrification

And about $1.2 trillion goes to oil, natural gas & coal: https://t.co/8IajZj1gIL https://t.co/9SrWhBJOx8
posts.summary: Global energy spending is projected to reach $3.4 trillion in 2026, with approximately $1.2 trillion allocated to oil, natural gas, and coal combined. This represents roughly 35% of total energy investment, with the remaining $2.2 trillion directed toward clean energy infrastructure and technologies.

posts.text: EIA: Miserable report for refiners with 3.1 m build in distillate stocks and 2.1-m build in gasoline. Gas demand measurement of 8.775-mbd will restart demand destruction dialogue. But refiners, blenders and importers are no longer bringing 10-mbd of gas to market, so be careful.
posts.summary: The EIA reported significant builds in distillate and gasoline inventories alongside weakening gasoline demand of 8.775 million barrels per day, signaling refiner challenges and potential demand destruction. With refiners and importers no longer supplying the typical 10 million barrels per day of gasoline to market, tight supply dynamics could support prices despite the demand weakness.

posts.text: Share prices for two of America's largest gasoline retailers, $MUSA and $CASY, are down 5-6 percent this morning after President Trump threatened to use the Department of Justice to investigate gasoline prices.
posts.summary: Major U.S. gasoline retailers Murphy USA and Casey's saw their stock prices decline 5-6% following President Trump's threat to use the Department of Justice to investigate gasoline prices. The market reaction suggests investor concern about potential regulatory scrutiny of fuel pricing practices.

posts.text: Russia’s Moscow Oil Refinery may remain offline until 2027 after suffering extensive damage from Ukrainian drone strikes. The facility normally supplies nearly half of Moscow’s fuel needs, adding pressure to a domestic market already facing shortages and rationing. #Russia
posts.summary: The Moscow Oil Refinery's potential offline status until 2027 following Ukrainian drone strikes could significantly tighten Russia's domestic fuel supply, given the facility normally provides nearly half of Moscow's fuel needs and the market is already experiencing shortages and rationing.

posts.text: Norway’s crude oil production beat official forecasts by 7.2% in May, reinforcing its role as one of Europe’s most reliable energy suppliers. Despite lower output versus April, year-to-date petroleum production remains 4% above 2025 levels. #Norway #Oil #NaturalGas #EnergyMarkets
posts.summary: Norway's crude oil production exceeded forecasts by 7.2% in May, demonstrating its continued reliability as a European energy supplier, with year-to-date output running 4% above 2025 levels despite a monthly decline from April.

posts.text:
Oil Prices Retreat to Pre-War Level as Supply Concerns Ease

Crude oil prices and calendar spreads have largely reverted to pre-war levels as traders anticipate a rapid normalisation of exports through the Strait of Hormuz and the smooth replenishment of depleted oil inventories https://t.co/kQu7Q9hU3r
posts.summary: Crude oil prices have declined to pre-war levels as traders expect normalized exports through the Strait of Hormuz and replenishment of depleted inventories to ease supply concerns. The market's retreat in both spot prices and calendar spreads reflects reduced geopolitical risk premium in oil valuations.

posts.text:
East Asian refiners seek sanctions clarity before buying Iranian crude

🔷 South Korea, Japan, Thai, Taiwan refiners seek sanctions clarity
🔷 Refinery configuration adjustments needed for Iranian crude
🔷 Platts assessed South Pars at $5.11/b discount to DFC
posts.summary: East Asian refiners are hesitant to purchase Iranian crude until receiving clearer guidance on sanctions regulations, while those willing to proceed would need to modify their refinery operations to process the oil, which is currently trading at a $5.11 per barrel discount to Dubai Fateh Crude.
